seo.title: Healthcare Content Generation AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for content generation AI agents in healthcare. Covers HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, HITECH Act.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for healthcare content generation carries unique risks where inaccurate medical information, PHI exposure, or non-compliant outputs can result in patient harm, malpractice liability, and HIPAA violations up to $1.5M per incident. This checklist ensures your LLM-powered content pipeline meets HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, and HITECH Act requirements while maintaining brand integrity and clinical accuracy. Every item is scoped specifically to the intersection of regulated healthcare environments and AI-assisted editorial workflows.

sections.heading: PHI Protection & Data Governance
sections.description: Ensure all patient health information is rigorously controlled before, during, and after LLM processing to satisfy HIPAA and HITECH Act mandates.

items.label: PHI De-identification Pipeline
items.description: Implement an automated de-identification layer using HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination method that strips all 18 PHI identifiers from any source content before it reaches the LLM inference endpoint. Log every transformation with a timestamp and operator ID.

items.label: Data Residency Enforcement
items.description: Configure your LLM deployment (self-hosted or private cloud) to ensure patient-adjacent data never leaves your defined processing environment. Disable all external API calls, telemetry, and model training data uploads in the LLM runtime configuration.

items.label: Business Associate Agreement (BAA) Verification
items.description: Confirm signed BAAs are in place with every vendor in the content generation stack — including LLM providers, vector database vendors, and cloud infrastructure providers — before processing any content that references patient populations or clinical scenarios.

items.label: Content Input Sanitization
items.description: Deploy a pre-processing filter that scans all editorial briefs, source documents, and user prompts for accidental PHI inclusion (e.g., real patient names, MRNs, dates of service) and blocks or redacts them before LLM submission.

items.label: Encryption at Rest and In Transit
items.description: Enforce AES-256 encryption for all stored content drafts, prompt logs, and output artifacts. Require TLS 1.3 for all data in transit between editorial tools, the LLM API, and downstream CMS platforms.

items.label: Minimum Necessary Access Controls
items.description: Apply role-based access control (RBAC) so that content editors, compliance reviewers, and LLM system accounts each have the minimum data access required for their function. Audit access logs monthly.

items.label: Data Retention and Deletion Policy
items.description: Define and enforce a documented retention schedule for LLM prompt history, generated drafts, and review logs. Automate deletion of content artifacts containing any clinical context after the defined retention window, with deletion receipts stored for audit.

sections.heading: Clinical Accuracy & Fact-Checking Controls
sections.description: Establish verification workflows that prevent medically inaccurate content from being published, reducing malpractice exposure and protecting patient safety.

items.label: Mandatory Clinical SME Review Gate
items.description: Require a licensed clinician (MD, NP, PharmD, or relevant specialist) to review and digitally sign off on all AI-generated content containing clinical claims, drug information, dosage guidance, or diagnostic criteria before publication. This approval must be logged with the reviewer's credentials and timestamp.

items.label: Automated Medical Claim Flagging
items.description: Integrate a post-generation NLP classifier that identifies and flags specific medical claims (e.g., efficacy statements, contraindications, treatment recommendations) in LLM output for mandatory human review before the content advances in the editorial workflow.

items.label: Citation and Source Verification
items.description: Require the LLM to output citations for all clinical facts, then run an automated verification step that checks each citation against approved source databases (PubMed, FDA drug labels, CDC guidelines). Flag uncited or unverifiable claims for editor resolution.

items.label: Outdated Medical Guideline Detection
items.description: Maintain a versioned knowledge cutoff registry for your LLM and cross-reference generated content against a curated list of recently updated clinical guidelines (e.g., AHA, ADA, WHO). Alert editors when content references superseded protocols.

items.label: Drug Interaction and Contraindication Screening
items.description: For any content mentioning medications, run generated text through an approved drug interaction API (e.g., NLM RxNorm, DrFirst) to detect potentially harmful or inaccurate statements about drug combinations or contraindications.

items.label: Disclaimer and Scope-of-Content Enforcement
items.description: Configure a post-processing rule engine to automatically append required medical disclaimers (e.g., 'This content is for informational purposes only and does not constitute medical advice') to all consumer-facing AI-generated health content, and verify their presence before CMS publish.

items.label: Hallucination Rate Monitoring
items.description: Establish a baseline hallucination rate metric for your LLM on healthcare content tasks. Run weekly red-team evaluations using a held-out set of clinical questions with known correct answers, and trigger a model review if hallucination rate exceeds your defined threshold (e.g., >2%).

sections.heading: Auditability, Logging & FDA 21 CFR Part 11 Compliance
sections.description: Implement tamper-evident audit trails and electronic record controls required by FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and HIPAA to ensure every AI-assisted content decision is fully reconstructable.

items.label: Immutable Audit Log for All LLM Interactions
items.description: Log every prompt submitted to the LLM, the full model output, model version, temperature settings, and the authenticated user ID in an append-only, tamper-evident log store (e.g., AWS CloudTrail with S3 Object Lock or equivalent). Retain logs for a minimum of 6 years per HIPAA requirements.

items.label: Electronic Signature Controls for Content Approval
items.description: Implement 21 CFR Part 11-compliant electronic signatures for all clinical review approvals, editorial sign-offs, and compliance checkpoints. Each signature must capture the signer's full name, date/time, and the meaning of the signature (e.g., 'Reviewed for clinical accuracy').

items.label: Version-Controlled Content Lineage
items.description: Maintain a complete version history for every piece of AI-generated content, linking each version to the specific LLM model version, prompt template version, and human edits applied. Store this lineage in your content management system with read-only access for auditors.

items.label: Audit Trail Integrity Verification
items.description: Schedule automated weekly integrity checks on audit log files using cryptographic hash verification to detect any unauthorized modification or deletion. Alert the compliance team immediately if integrity checks fail.

items.label: Change Control Documentation for Prompt Templates
items.description: Treat LLM prompt templates as controlled documents under your change management process. Require documented justification, compliance review, and approval before any prompt template modification, and version-stamp all templates used in production.

items.label: User Authentication and Session Logging
items.description: Enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all users accessing the content generation platform. Log all login events, session durations, and content actions (create, edit, approve, publish) with user identity and IP address.

items.label: Automated Compliance Report Generation
items.description: Configure your platform to generate monthly compliance summary reports showing total AI-generated content volume, clinical review completion rates, flagged content counts, and audit log integrity status. Distribute to the compliance officer and retain for regulatory inspection readiness.

sections.heading: Brand Voice, Editorial Quality & Content Governance
sections.description: Enforce brand consistency, tone adherence, originality, and SEO compliance across all AI-generated healthcare content while maintaining editorial standards appropriate for a regulated medical publisher.

items.label: Brand Voice Style Guide Integration
items.description: Encode your organization's brand voice guidelines (tone, reading level, preferred terminology, prohibited phrases) as a structured system prompt or retrieval-augmented context document. Run automated style scoring on every LLM output against these guidelines before editorial review.

items.label: Health Literacy Level Enforcement
items.description: Configure a post-generation readability check using Flesch-Kincaid or SMOG scoring to ensure consumer-facing content meets your target health literacy level (typically 6th–8th grade for patient-facing materials per CDC plain language guidelines). Flag content that scores outside the acceptable range.

items.label: Originality and Plagiarism Screening
items.description: Run all AI-generated content through a plagiarism detection tool (e.g., Copyscape, iThenticate) before publication. Set a maximum similarity threshold (e.g., <10% match) and require editor review for any content exceeding it, documenting the resolution.

items.label: Prohibited Medical Claims Blocklist
items.description: Maintain and enforce a regularly updated blocklist of prohibited phrases — including unsubstantiated efficacy claims, off-label drug promotion language, and FDA-regulated disease cure claims — as a post-processing filter that prevents non-compliant content from advancing to publication.

items.label: SEO Compliance and E-E-A-T Alignment
items.description: Validate that all published AI-generated health content meets Google's E-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) standards by including verified author credentials, publication dates, last-reviewed dates, and links to authoritative sources. Automate a pre-publish checklist for these elements.

items.label: Tone and Sensitivity Review for Patient-Facing Content
items.description: Implement a secondary review step for any content addressing sensitive health topics (mental health, oncology, reproductive health, substance use) to ensure language is empathetic, non-stigmatizing, and aligned with current patient advocacy standards (e.g., person-first language).

items.label: Content Expiration and Review Scheduling
items.description: Assign a mandatory review expiration date (maximum 12 months) to all published AI-generated clinical content. Automate alerts to content owners 30 days before expiration to trigger re-review against current clinical guidelines before the content is either updated or unpublished.

items.label: AI Disclosure Labeling
items.description: Implement a policy and automated enforcement mechanism to label all AI-assisted content with a standardized disclosure (e.g., 'This content was created with AI assistance and reviewed by a licensed clinician') in compliance with emerging FTC guidance on AI-generated content transparency.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR §164.514) requires that all 18 PHI identifiers be removed before content containing patient-derived information is processed by any third-party LLM. Using the Safe Harbor de-identification method is the lowest-risk approach; Expert Determination requires documented statistical justification and a qualified expert's signed attestation.

FDA 21 CFR Part 11 applies if your AI-generated content is used in clinical trial documentation, adverse event reporting, or any submission to the FDA. In these cases, your LLM platform must meet electronic records and electronic signature requirements, including audit trails, access controls, and system validation documentation (IQ/OQ/PQ protocols).

The HITECH Act strengthens HIPAA enforcement and extends liability to Business Associates, meaning your LLM vendor, cloud provider, and any analytics tools in the content pipeline are directly liable for breaches. Ensure BAAs explicitly cover AI processing activities and that your vendor's SOC 2 Type II report covers the specific services used in your content workflow.

FTC Act Section 5 and emerging state-level AI transparency laws (e.g., Colorado AI Act, California SB 1047 successor legislation) may require disclosure when AI is used to generate health-related content distributed to consumers. Proactively implement AI authorship disclosure labels on all published content to reduce regulatory exposure as this landscape evolves.

cta.heading: Automate Your Healthcare Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI content pipeline against HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, and HITECH requirements — so your team ships compliant healthcare content faster without manual checklist overhead.
